description: This figure presents scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images showing the multiphase microstructure evolution of Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si low-density steel during rolling and heat treatment processes. The eight subfigures display: (a) hot-rolled (HR) state showing lath-like fresh martensite (FM) and bainite (B); (b) cold-rolled state with elongated lamellar structure containing FM and B; (c-e) intercritical annealing at 750°C (AN750), 800°C (AN800), and 850°C (AN850) revealing polygonal ferrite (F), bainite (B), fresh martensite (FM), and retained austenite (RA, indicated by red arrows); and (f-h) corresponding annealed samples with additional low bainite aging at 400°C (AN750-T, AN800-T, AN850-T) showing tempered martensite (TM) formation alongside FM, F, and B. The images, captured at magnifications indicated by 5-10 μm scale bars, demonstrate the heterogeneous microstructure evolution from deformed lath structures to multiphase mixtures, with phase fractions and morphologies varying systematically with annealing temperature. The coordinate system indicates normal direction (ND) and rolling direction (RD).
